description: Declarative rendering + prompt-shaping rules for Icon Maker. Edited as content — no Python rebuild needed. Loaded by wisdom.py at render time; explicit caller fields always win over wisdom suggestions.

compositional.why: Pale icons vanish on a white page. A soft 2px stroke gives a crisp edge without visual weight.
Light-bg icons look pasted-on without a subtle drop shadow beneath the glyph.
Bright glyph on near-black reads as flat. A soft glow in the symbol color makes it pop without looking neon.
If symbol:bg contrast is below WCAG AA Large, flip symbol to pure white (dark bg) or charcoal #111827 (light bg).
Medium-dark backgrounds sometimes land in a mushy contrast zone. A subtle shadow separates the glyph without a full contrast-flip.

prompt_shaping.why: Two colors with near-identical luminance produce a muddy gradient. Darken the second stop so the glyph reads.
Two full-saturation colors clash (e.g. cyan + magenta both at 1.0). Anchor one to near-black so the neon has contrast.
Games should feel vibrant + playful by default, not flat or minimal.
Utility/productivity apps read better as clean flat tiles than gradients with glows.
If parser picked a symbol color near the background color, fall back to white (dark bg) or charcoal (light bg).
Dark gradients need a pure-white symbol, not #F5E6C8 or similar off-whites that muddy the contrast.
